overview.summary: Stacks are simple LIFO (Last-In-First-Out) data structures, like a stack of plates.
learningObjectives: Learn to use stacks for reversing and nesting
overview.interviewRelevance: Essential for parsing tasks like parenthesis matching and tree traversal.

paragraphs: Your CPU maintains a call stack right now. Every function call pushes a stack frame. Every return pops it.
When you get a StackOverflowError, the call stack physically ran out of memory.
The brackets validator in your IDE uses an explicit stack to track nesting.
realWorldHook.ahaMoment: A stack perfectly models 'returning to where I was recently' because the last thing pushed is the first thing popped.

definition.technicalDefinition: A stack is a linear data structure that follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle, meaning elements are added and removed from the same end (the 'top').

vocabulary.definition: Add an element to the top of the stack.
Remove the element from the top of the stack.
Look at the top element without removing it.

keyProperties: Elements are added and removed in O(1) time.
Random access to elements in the middle is not allowed.
LIFO behavior inherently reverses the order of items.

operations.name: Push
operations.description: Place an item on the top of the stack.
operations.visualNote: A new block appears exactly on top of the pile.

operations.name: Pop
operations.description: Remove the top item from the stack.

operations.name: Peek
operations.description: Return the top item without removing it.
operations.visualNote: The topmost block is highlighted, but remains in place.

practice.title: Valid Parentheses
practice.keyInsight: Push openers, pop closers to verify match.

practice.title: Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
practice.keyInsight: Operands go on the stack, operators pop two and push one.

practice.title: Daily Temperatures
practice.keyInsight: Monotonic decreasing stack to resolve 'next greater' queries.

practice.title: Implement Queue using Stacks
practice.keyInsight: Pour from an 'in' stack to an 'out' stack.
